response:
# Verdict: REJECT

## Overall Assessment

This expandable section has **critical accessibility design failures** that block keyboard and assistive technology access entirely. The component uses a `<div>` with only `onClick` handler—no semantic button, no ARIA state communication, no keyboard support. This is not an "80% implemented" pattern; it's fundamentally inaccessible by design.

## Pre-commitment Predictions (vs Actual)

| Prediction | Status | Notes |
|------------|--------|-------|
| Missing role="button" or native button element | ✓ Confirmed | CRITICAL finding as predicted |
| No aria-expanded state communication | ✓ Confirmed | MAJOR/CRITICAL depending on severity calibration |
| No keyboard handler (Space/Enter) | ✓ Confirmed | Blocks keyboard-only access entirely |
| Focus management undefined | ✓ Confirmed | Tab order exists but activation fails |

**Surprise**: None—this component has all the classic disclosure widget anti-patterns I predicted. The visual indicator (+/-) is present, which makes it worse: users see something clickable that AT cannot interact with at all.

---

## Critical Findings (Blocks Access Entirely)

### 1. Interactive Element Without Semantic Button or ARIA Role
```jsx
// File: ExpandableSection.tsx
// Line: ~8-20 (div element with onClick but no role/keyboard support)
<div className="expandable-trigger" onClick={toggle}>
```

**Evidence**: The interactive trigger is a `<div>` without `role="button"` or native button semantics. No keyboard handler for Space/Enter activation.

| Attribute | Expected Value | Actual Value | WCAG/APG Citation |
|-----------|-----------------|---------------|-------------------|
| Element type | `<button>` OR `<div role="button">` with keyboard support | `<div>` only | WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value; WAI-ARIA Disclosure Pattern |

**User Group Impacted**: Keyboard-only users (WCAG 2.1.1), Screen reader users who cannot activate the control at all.

**Why This Matters**: A screen reader user hears "Expandable Section" but has no way to interact with it using keyboard or AT commands. The `onClick` handler only works for mouse/touch, not keyboard (Space/Enter).

**Fix**: Replace `<div>` with native `<button>`, OR add `role="button"` AND implement keyboard event handlers:
```jsx
<button 
  className="expandable-trigger"
  onClick={toggle}
  onKeyDown={(e) => { if ([' ', 'Enter'].includes(e.key)) toggle(); }}
  aria-expanded={isExpanded}
>
  {title}
</button>
```

---

### 2. Missing aria-expanded State Communication
```jsx
// File: ExpandableSection.tsx  
// Line: ~19 (no aria-expanded attribute)
<div className="expandable-trigger" onClick={toggle}>
```

**Evidence**: No `aria-expanded` or `aria-controls` attributes present to communicate expansion state. The visual +/- indicator is NOT sufficient for AT users per WCAG 4.1.2 and APG Disclosure pattern requirements.

| Attribute | Expected Value | Actual Value | Impact |
|-----------|-----------------|---------------|--------|
| aria-expanded | `"true"` when expanded, `"false"` otherwise | Missing entirely | State not announced to screen readers |

**User Group Impacted**: Screen reader users cannot know if content is currently visible without navigating into it. This breaks the disclosure interaction model completely.

**Why This Matters**: Per WAI-ARIA APG Disclosure pattern, `aria-expanded` MUST toggle with state changes so AT announces "expanded" or "collapsed". Without this, screen readers announce identical text for both states—confusing and unusable.

**Fix**: Add `aria-expanded={isExpanded}` to the trigger element:
```jsx
<button 
  aria-expanded={isExpanded}
  onClick={() => setIsExpanded(!prevIsExpanded)} // Use functional update or ref-based toggle
>
```

---

### 3. No Keyboard Activation Handler (Space/Enter)
```jsx
// File: ExpandableSection.tsx  
// Line: ~8-20 (only onClick handler present, no onKeyDown/onKeyPress)
<div className="expandable-trigger" onClick={toggle}>
```

**Evidence**: Only `onClick` event listener. No keyboard activation for Space/Enter keys. This is a WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard violation—keyboard users cannot activate the control at all.

| Event | Expected Handler | Actual Implementation | WCAG Citation |
|-------|------------------|----------------------|---------------|
| onKeyDown/onKeyPress | Toggle on 'Space' or 'Enter' | Missing entirely | WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard (Level A) |

**User Group Impacted**: All keyboard-only users, including those who cannot use mouse/touch devices at all.

**Why This Matters**: Per WCAG 2.1.1, "All functionality available using a mouse is also available from a keyboard." The current implementation fails this requirement entirely—keyboard users literally cannot expand/collapse the section without tabbing out and back in repeatedly (workaround that's not acceptable).

**Fix**: Add onKeyDown handler:
```jsx
<button 
  onClick={toggle}
  onKeyDown={(e) => {
    if ([' ', 'Enter'].includes(e.key)) {
      e.preventDefault(); // Prevent default space scroll behavior
      toggle();
    }
  }}
>
```

---

## Major Findings (Significantly Degrades Experience)

### 4. Visual Indicator Not Programmatic (+/- Characters)
```jsx
// File: ExpandableSection.tsx  
// Line: ~20-21 (visual +/- text rendered directly in DOM)
{isExpanded ? ' −' : '+'}
```

**Evidence**: The expansion state is indicated visually via `+`/`−` characters but NOT programmatically. Per WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages, visual-only indicators are insufficient—AT users need programmatic state communication (aria-expanded). Additionally, these Unicode symbols may be announced as "plus" or "minus" rather than being recognized as expansion/collapse indicators without proper context.

**User Group Impacted**: Screen reader users who cannot distinguish expanded vs collapsed states visually.

**Why This Matters**: The visual indicator is redundant if aria-expanded exists (which it doesn't). If you remove the +/- and rely on aria-expanded, AT will announce "expanded" or "collapsed"—clearer than hearing "+". Per WCAG 1.4.2 Audio Control guidance:
